topic.what: Foundational Ayurvedic text containing the earliest known description of amlapitta (acid gastritis) as a disorder of excess pitta dosha.
topic.how: Constitutional diagnosis via dosha assessment; treatment through pitta-pacifying diet, cooling herbs (Shatavari, Yashtimadhu, Amalaki), and lifestyle modification.
topic.why_matters: Provides a complete independent medical tradition's framework for the same disease entity Western medicine named 'gastritis' — enabling cross-paradigm comparison of diagnostic and therapeutic approaches.

characteristics.what_it_measures: How specifically the condition is defined and differentiated
characteristics.value: Amlapitta clearly distinguished from other GI conditions via pitta/agni assessment
characteristics.vs_baseline: More specific gastric classification than Hippocratic corpus

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of specific herbal interventions documented
characteristics.value: 20+ specific gastroprotective herbs with preparation methods

good_for: Understanding constitutional approaches to GI disease

novelty: Earliest known clinical description of acid gastritis (amlapitta)
Complete constitutional framework linking body type to gastric disease susceptibility
Integrated diet-herb-lifestyle approach predating modern integrative medicine by 2000 years
Concept of agni (digestive fire) parallels modern understanding of gastric acid homeostasis
open_problems: No controlled clinical validation of traditional protocols
Translation challenges affect interpretation of specific recommendations
Dosha framework not mappable to modern biomarkers
